metadata.subject: Applied Design, Skills and Technologies
metadata.description: Students develop advanced media design skills encompassing brand identity systems, campaign design, and industry-standard practices. This course emphasizes strategic design thinking, visual communication, and professional portfolio development for media design careers.
metadata.general_description: Advanced media design, brand identity systems, campaign design, and industry standards.

big_ideas.statement: Media design communicates messages and builds identity through strategic visual choices
big_ideas.elaboration: Effective design combines aesthetic principles with strategic communication to achieve intended outcomes.

big_ideas.statement: Brand identity systems create consistent visual communication across diverse applications and media
big_ideas.elaboration: Comprehensive brand systems ensure recognition, coherence, and professional communication across touchpoints.

big_ideas.statement: Campaign design integrates multiple media and messages to achieve strategic marketing objectives
big_ideas.elaboration: Successful campaigns coordinate messages across channels while adapting to medium-specific requirements.

big_ideas.statement: Professional media design requires understanding audience, context, technology, and design principles
big_ideas.elaboration: Designers must balance creative vision with audience needs, technical constraints, and strategic objectives.

i_can_statements: I can develop brand strategies and positioning
I can design logo marks and wordmarks
I can create color palettes and typography systems
I can develop brand guidelines and applications
I can develop campaign concepts and themes
I can design across multiple media and formats
I can adapt designs for different channels
I can create cohesive campaign communications
I can analyze communication objectives
I can develop visual strategies
I can create information design solutions
I can design for different audiences and contexts
I can design intuitive user interfaces
I can plan user journeys and interactions
I can design for accessibility and usability
I can create interactive prototypes

competencies.raw: Apply design thinking and strategy to creative problems
i_can_statements: I can research and analyze target audiences
I can develop design concepts strategically
I can present design rationales
I can refine designs through feedback

competencies.raw: Apply design principles to create compelling visual communications
i_can_statements: I can apply balance, contrast, and hierarchy
I can use typography strategically
I can develop color systems effectively
I can create visual impact

competencies.raw: Apply industry standards and best practices
i_can_statements: I can create production-ready designs
I can follow design standards and guidelines
I can manage files and specifications
I can work within technical constraints

competencies.raw: Apply project management and client communication
i_can_statements: I can manage design projects
I can communicate with clients and stakeholders
I can present designs professionally
I can incorporate feedback effectively

competencies.raw: Use professional design software and tools
i_can_statements: I can use vector and raster design applications
I can use layout and publishing tools
I can use 3D and animation software
I can integrate multiple software tools

competencies.raw: Use prototyping and interactive design tools
i_can_statements: I can create interactive prototypes
I can use wireframing and mockup tools
I can use animation and interaction tools
I can test and iterate designs

competencies.raw: Use web and digital design technologies
i_can_statements: I can use web design and development tools
I can create responsive designs
I can optimize designs for different platforms
I can deploy digital designs
I can create digital portfolios
I can present designs professionally
I can use presentation software
I can document and showcase work
